title: Dynamic Glazing Visible Light Transmittance Levels
summary: Electrochromic dynamic glazing typically offers four tint darkness levels corresponding to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance (Tvis).
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Orientation Applicability
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades that do not face north; it works well on east and west orientations and on unshaded south facades.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not do not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Dynamic Glazing Control Methods
summary: Dynamic glazing can be controlled manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

structured_payload.control_method: Manual switch or BMS automatic control

evidence_quote: The dynamic glazing is usually controlled by a low voltage current and can be controlled either manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

title: Active Desiccant Dehumidification Dew Point Threshold
summary: Applications requiring a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.
evidence_quote: In general, applications that require a dew point at or below 40° F (4.3° C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.

title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Airflow Operation
summary: In rotary desiccant dehumidifiers, humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while a separate reactivation air stream dries another part; the desiccant rotates slowly between these airstreams.

structured_payload.process: Desiccant rotation between process and reactivation airstreams

evidence_quote: Humid air, generally referred to as process air, is dehumidified in one part of the desiccant bed while a different part of the bed is dried for reuse by a second airstream known as reactivation air.

title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Process Air Outlet Temperature Rise
summary: The process air outlet temperature from a rotary desiccant dehumidifier rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture removed, primarily due to heat of sorption.

structured_payload.performance_characteristic: Outlet temperature rise proportional to moisture removal

evidence_quote: The process air outlet temperature is higher than the inlet temperature primarily because the heat of sorption of the moisture removed is converted to sensible heat.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Application Examples
summary: Candidates for active desiccant dehumidification include facilities handling hygroscopic materials; film drying; candy, chocolate, chewing gum, drug, chemical, plastic, and electronics manufacturing; moisture-sensitive product packaging; and supermarkets to avoid condensation on refrigerated casework.

structured_payload.application_examples: Hygroscopic materials, film drying, food manufacturing, drug/chemical/plastic/electronics manufacturing, moisture-sensitive packaging, supermarkets

evidence_quote: Examples of such candidates include facilities handling hygroscopic materials; film drying; the manufacturing of candy, chocolate, or chewing gum; the manufacturing of drugs and chemicals; the manufacturing of plastic materials; packaging of moisture-sensitive products; and the manufacturing of electronics.

title: Desiccant Reactivation Temperature Strategy
summary: For rotary desiccant dehumidifiers, higher reactivation air inlet temperatures are generally used when drier outlet air is needed, while low-grade heat sources (e.g., waste heat) can be used when leaving humidity need not be especially low.

structured_payload.strategy: Higher temps for drier air, lower temps acceptable for less stringent humidity requirements

evidence_quote: In turn, if the designer needs dry air, it is generally more economical to use high reactivation temperatures. On the other hand, if the leaving humidity need not be especially low, inexpensive, low-grade heat sources can be used.
